It just goes to show that even the smallest act of kindness can completely brighten someone's day.

title: A Time You Felt Extremely Nervous
prompt_text: Everyone has moments when their nerves get the better of them. Talk about a time when you felt extremely nervous about something. When and where did this happen? Who were you with, and what made you so nervous? How did you feel afterward?
template_hint: Set the scene (when, where, who) -> describe the nervous build-up and what actually happened -> end with how you felt once it was over and why it stuck with you.
sample_response_band12: A few years ago, I had to give my very first presentation at a new job, and I was standing outside the meeting room with about twenty colleagues waiting inside. At first I felt fairly calm, but as the clock ticked closer my heart started racing and my hands were trembling. I kept rehearsing my opening line over and over, terrified I'd forget everything. Then the manager called my name, and before I knew it I was at the front, my voice a little shaky. Honestly, the first thirty seconds were a blur. But once I got into my rhythm, the nerves slowly melted away and people started nodding along. In the end, my boss congratulated me and I felt absolutely relieved and even a bit proud. Looking back, that nerve-racking moment taught me that I'm braver than I think.

title: A Time You Had To Be Brave
prompt_text: Sometimes life asks us to find courage we did not know we had. Talk about a time when you had to be brave. What was the situation, and where did it take place? Why did it require courage from you? How did things turn out in the end?
template_hint: Set the scene and the challenge -> explain why it was frightening and how you summoned courage -> finish with the outcome and what it meant to you.
sample_response_band12: It happened a couple of winters ago, when my elderly neighbour slipped on the ice right outside our building and couldn't get up. To be honest, I froze for a second because there was nobody else around and I had no idea what to do. At that point I realised I was the only one who could help, so I decided to stay calm, call an ambulance, and keep her warm with my own coat until it arrived. It was daunting, because she was frightened and in pain, and I was terrified of making things worse. So I just kept talking to her gently and reassuring her. Eventually the paramedics came and told me I'd done exactly the right thing. Looking back, that quiet act of courage made me realise that being brave isn't about feeling fearless. It's about acting anyway.

title: When You Stood Up For Someone
prompt_text: There are moments when we feel we have to speak up, either for ourselves or for someone else. Talk about a time when you stood up for yourself or another person. What happened, and where did it take place? Why did you feel you had to step in, and what was the result of your actions?
template_hint: Set the scene and the unfair situation -> explain your hesitation and the moment you decided to act -> end with the outcome and the values it confirmed.
sample_response_band12: One day at my part-time job in a cafe, I watched a customer rudely shouting at a new colleague over a tiny mistake with his order. At first I hesitated, because the customer was older and clearly furious, and I didn't want to make a scene. But then I saw my coworker on the verge of tears, and I just couldn't stay silent. So I stepped in calmly, apologized for the mix-up, offered to remake his drink, and politely said there was no need to raise his voice. He went quiet, muttered something, and eventually left. After that, my coworker thanked me again and again, and even the manager noticed. In hindsight, confronting that situation was uncomfortable, but it felt absolutely right. It reminded me that staying silent in the face of unfairness just isn't who I want to be.

title: A Big Risk You Decided To Take
prompt_text: Sometimes we take a chance that could change the direction of our lives. Talk about a time when you decided to take a big risk. What was the risk, and what was going on in your life at the time? Why did you decide to go for it, and what happened as a result?
template_hint: Set the scene and the stakes -> explain the doubts, the decision to leap, and what unfolded -> finish with the payoff and what you learned about taking chances.
sample_response_band12: A while back, I had a stable, comfortable office job, but deep down I'd always dreamed of moving abroad to study. So when I got accepted into a program in another country, I faced a huge decision, because saying yes meant quitting my job, leaving my family, and emptying most of my savings. After a lot of thought and quite a few sleepless nights, I decided to take the leap. Despite all the doubts and the warnings from relatives, something told me I'd regret it forever if I didn't try. Ultimately, I packed two suitcases and flew off into total uncertainty. As it turned out, that gamble completely transformed my life. I gained new skills, lifelong friends, and a confidence I never had before. Looking back now, it was the scariest yet best decision I've ever made, and the payoff was more than worth the risk.

title: A Time You Faced Something Alone
prompt_text: Occasionally we have to handle a difficult situation entirely on our own. Talk about a time when you had to face something alone. What was the challenge, when did it happen, and why was there no one to help you? How did you cope, and how did you feel once it was over?
connector_phrases: With no one to turn to
template_hint: Set the scene and why you were alone -> describe how you coped step by step with the challenge -> close with the relief and the strength you discovered in yourself.
sample_response_band12: It was around the time I first moved to a new city for work, completely on my own and knowing absolutely no one. One night, just two weeks in, I came down with a high fever and could barely get out of bed. With no family nearby and no friends yet to call, I felt utterly isolated and honestly a little scared. But step by step, I forced myself to manage. I ordered medicine online, made simple soup, and rested as much as I could. Somehow, despite feeling miserable, I got myself through three difficult days. After a while the fever finally broke, and I remember lying there feeling exhausted but strangely proud. In the end, facing that alone taught me how resilient and self-reliant I really could be. It was a turning point that made me trust myself like never before.

title: A Memorable Day At Work
prompt_text: Talk about a memorable day you had at work. Describe a specific day that stands out clearly in your memory. When did this happen, and where were you working? What made the day so memorable for you?
template_hint: Set the scene (when, where, your role), then walk through what happened during the day step by step, and finish with why the day stayed with you.
sample_response_band12: A couple of years ago, I had a day at work I'll never forget. I was working as a sales assistant at a small electronics shop downtown, and it was the day before the big holiday sale. At first, the morning was completely chaotic because three of my coworkers had called in sick, so I was practically running the store on my own. Then customers kept pouring in, and I somehow managed to keep everyone happy. By closing time, I had sold more than anyone in the store's history that day. My manager came over, shook my hand, and told the whole team how proud she was of me. I felt absolutely exhausted but incredibly proud of myself. Looking back, that day really stands out because it showed me I could stay calm and handle real pressure, and that feeling has stayed with me ever since.

title: A Team Project You Worked On
prompt_text: Talk about a time when you worked on a project together with a team. Choose a specific project where you cooperated closely with other people. What was the project about, and who else was involved? How did the team work together, and how did things turn out?
template_hint: Introduce the project and the team, then explain how you divided the work and faced challenges together, and close with the result and how the teamwork felt.
sample_response_band12: A few months ago, I worked on a group project with four classmates for our marketing course. The project was about designing a complete advertising campaign for a local coffee shop, and we only had three weeks to finish it. To begin with, we sat down and divided the responsibilities, so I took charge of the social media side while the others handled research and design. As we went along, we ran into disagreements about the colours and the slogan, but we always talked things through and reached a compromise. Meanwhile, we met almost every evening online to check our progress. In the end, our campaign won the highest grade in the class, and the coffee shop owner actually used some of our ideas. All in all, it was an amazing experience because I learned how much a team can achieve when everyone pulls in the same direction.

title: Solving A Tricky Problem
prompt_text: Talk about a time when you solved a tricky problem at work or at school. Describe a specific situation where something was difficult to figure out. What was the problem, and how did you finally solve it? How did you feel afterward?
template_hint: Describe the problem and why it was difficult, then explain the steps you took to work it out, and end with the solution and your sense of relief or pride.
sample_response_band12: Last year, I ran into a really tricky problem while working as a junior accountant. One afternoon, our monthly report wouldn't balance, and there was a difference of nearly two thousand dollars that nobody could explain. At first, I assumed it was a simple typing mistake, so I checked every entry twice, but the numbers still didn't add up. After a while, I started to feel quite frustrated because the deadline was the next morning. Eventually, I decided to trace the figures backwards, transaction by transaction. Finally, I discovered that one invoice had been recorded twice in two different accounts. Once I removed the duplicate, everything balanced perfectly. In the end, I felt an enormous sense of relief, and honestly, a bit of pride too. That experience taught me that staying patient and changing your approach is often the key to cracking a difficult problem.

title: A Colleague Who Really Helped
prompt_text: Talk about a colleague or classmate who really helped you. Describe a specific person who gave you support when you needed it. Who was this person, and what was your situation at the time? How exactly did they help you, and why do you still remember it?
template_hint: Introduce the person and the difficult moment you were in, then describe what they did to help you, and finish with how grateful you felt.
sample_response_band12: When I first started my job as a nurse, I felt completely overwhelmed, and there was one senior colleague named Maria who really helped me through it. At that time, I was terrified of making mistakes, and the hospital felt huge and confusing. One day, she noticed me struggling during a busy shift, and instead of letting me sink, she quietly stayed late to walk me through every procedure. She was incredibly patient and never made me feel stupid for asking questions. Thanks to her guidance, I gradually built up my confidence, and within a couple of months I was handling my own patients comfortably. From then on, she became a kind of mentor to me. To this day, I'm deeply grateful for her kindness, and whenever I see a new colleague struggling, I try to help them the way she helped me.

title: An Interview That Went Well
prompt_text: Talk about an interview or a test that went surprisingly well for you. Describe a specific time when you did much better than you expected. What was the interview or test for, and why were you worried about it? What happened, and how did you feel afterward?
template_hint: Set up the interview or test and your nervousness, then describe how it unexpectedly went smoothly, and close with your surprise and relief at the result.
sample_response_band12: A while back, I had a job interview for a position I really wanted, and to my surprise, it went far better than I ever expected. Beforehand, I was incredibly nervous because it was for a marketing role at a well-known company, and I assumed the competition would be fierce. The night before, I barely slept, and I kept imagining all the difficult questions they might ask. To my surprise, the moment I walked in, the two interviewers were warm and friendly, and we ended up having a genuine conversation rather than a tense interrogation. As it turned out, my preparation paid off, and I was able to answer every question confidently with real examples. In the end, they offered me the job on the spot. Looking back, it taught me that nervousness doesn't always predict the outcome, and good preparation truly makes a difference.

title: A Very Busy And Challenging Day
prompt_text: Talk about a specific day when you had an enormous amount to handle. Describe a time when everything seemed to happen at once and you felt under real pressure. When did it happen, and what made the day so demanding? How did you manage to get through it all?
connector_phrases: By the end of the day
template_hint: Set the scene of the day, then pile on the tasks and pressures you faced one after another, and finish with how you coped and how you felt at the end.
sample_response_band12: Not long ago, I had one of the most challenging days of my entire career. It was the day my company launched a new product, and I was responsible for coordinating everything. From the very start, my phone rang non-stop with questions from the team, and our website kept crashing because of all the traffic. On top of that, two key suppliers called to say their deliveries would be late, so I had to find replacements within an hour. I honestly felt completely overwhelmed and barely had time to eat lunch. Somehow, by staying focused and tackling one problem at a time, I managed to keep everything moving. By the end of the day, the launch was a success, and we had sold far more than we'd predicted. In hindsight, I was utterly exhausted, but I felt incredibly proud that I'd kept my cool under so much pressure.

title: A Time You Took The Lead
prompt_text: Talk about a time when you took the lead on something. Describe a specific situation where you stepped up and guided others. What was the situation, and why did you decide to take charge? What did you do, and how did it turn out?
template_hint: Explain the situation that needed a leader, then describe how you took charge and what actions you took, and close with the outcome and what leading taught you.
sample_response_band12: A couple of years ago, I took the lead on organizing a charity event for my community centre. Originally, someone else was supposed to run it, but he suddenly fell ill two weeks before the date. At that point, everything was a mess, with no volunteers confirmed and the venue still unbooked. So I decided to step up, even though I'd never led a project that big before. Step by step, I created a schedule, divided the tasks among twenty volunteers, and personally called every sponsor to keep them on board. I also worked hard to keep everyone motivated whenever people felt stressed. As a result, the event went ahead smoothly and we raised over five thousand dollars for the local food bank. In the end, I felt genuinely proud, and that experience showed me I was far more capable of leading people than I had ever realized.

title: An Important Lesson You Learned
prompt_text: Talk about an important lesson you learned at work or at school. Describe a specific experience from your past that taught you something valuable. What happened, and what exactly did you learn from it? How has that lesson affected you since then?
template_hint: Describe the experience and what went wrong or surprised you, then explain the lesson it taught you, and finish with how it has changed your behaviour since.
sample_response_band12: Years ago, when I was still a student, I learned a lesson that completely changed how I work. Back then, I was overconfident and often left my assignments until the very last minute. One time, I had a major research paper due, and I assumed I could finish it the night before. However, my computer crashed at midnight and I lost almost everything. I ended up submitting a rushed, messy paper and got the worst grade of my life. It was only later that I realized the problem wasn't bad luck, it was poor planning. That experience taught me the real value of preparing early and backing up my work. Ever since, I've started every important task well in advance, and these days my colleagues actually rely on me for being organized. That humbling moment honestly shaped the kind of professional I've become.

title: Fixing Something Yourself
prompt_text: Talk about a time when you fixed or built something yourself instead of paying someone else to do it. What was it, and what tools did you use? Was it harder than you expected, and how did you feel when you finally finished?
template_hint: Set the scene (what broke or needed building and why you decided to do it yourself), walk through the steps and the difficulties, then end with the sense of accomplishment you felt.
sample_response_band12: A while back, the bottom drawer of my kitchen cabinet completely came off its rails, and it was driving me crazy. Instead of calling a handyman, I decided to fix it myself one Saturday morning. To begin with, I watched a couple of short videos online to figure out what was actually wrong. Then I grabbed a screwdriver, a small hammer, and the spare screws I had lying around. Halfway through, I realized the rail itself was bent, so I had to carefully straighten it, which was honestly trickier than I expected. Eventually, after about an hour of trial and error, the drawer slid open and shut perfectly. In the end, I felt incredibly proud of myself. It was such a satisfying feeling, and now whenever I open that drawer, I quietly remind myself that I managed to fix it with my own two hands.

title: An Unforgettable Meal You Cooked
prompt_text: Describe a time when you cooked a meal that you will never forget. What did you make, and who were you cooking for? What made that meal so special, and how did the people react when they tasted it?
template_hint: Set the scene (the occasion, who you were cooking for), describe preparing the dish and the moment of serving it, then end with the reaction and why the meal stayed with you.
sample_response_band12: One evening last winter, I decided to cook a full three-course dinner for my parents' wedding anniversary. I wanted it to be special, so I chose my grandmother's old chicken curry recipe. First of all, I spent the whole afternoon chopping vegetables and grinding the spices from scratch, which filled the kitchen with an amazing aroma. While it was simmering, I quickly baked a simple chocolate cake for dessert. Just as my parents sat down, I brought everything to the table, feeling a little nervous. The moment they tasted the curry, my mom's eyes lit up, and my dad said it reminded him of his childhood. Finally, we all ate together, laughing and telling old stories. To this day, that meal stands out to me, not because of the food itself, but because of how genuinely happy it made everyone feel.

title: A Power Outage At Home
prompt_text: Talk about a time when something unexpected happened at home, such as a power outage. What were you doing when it happened, and how long did it last? How did you and the people around you deal with the situation?
template_hint: Set the scene (what you were doing and where), describe the sudden event and how you and others reacted, then end with how it turned out and what you took away from it.
sample_response_band12: One stormy night a few years ago, I was at home watching a movie with my younger sister while heavy rain pounded against the windows. Out of nowhere, the lights flickered twice and then the entire house went pitch dark. At that point, my sister got a little scared, so I tried to stay calm and reassure her. I fumbled around in the kitchen drawer until I found a flashlight, and then we lit a few candles in the living room. Meanwhile, my phone told me the outage had hit our whole neighbourhood. As time went on, we actually started enjoying ourselves, telling ghost stories and playing card games by candlelight. In the end, the power came back after about three hours. Honestly, that unexpected blackout turned into one of the cosiest, most memorable evenings I've ever spent with my sister.

title: A Weekend You Really Enjoyed
prompt_text: Think about a weekend that you really enjoyed. Tell a friend about it. Where did you go and who was with you? What did you do that made it so enjoyable, and why do you still remember it?
template_hint: Set the scene (when the weekend was, where you went, and who joined you), describe the main activities that made it special, then end with why it stayed memorable.
sample_response_band12: Last summer, I spent an absolutely wonderful weekend at a small lakeside cabin with three of my closest friends. We had all been really stressed from work, so we desperately needed to recharge. On the first day, we went swimming in the lake and then lay in the sun, just chatting and laughing about everything. After that, we cooked dinner together outside and watched the sunset paint the whole sky orange and pink. The next morning, we woke up early and went hiking through this quiet forest trail, with birds singing all around us. Before we knew it, the weekend was already over, which made us a little sad. Looking back, what made it so special wasn't really the activities themselves. It was the feeling of being completely carefree and surrounded by good friends. To this day, I think of it whenever I need to relax.

title: A Memorable Morning You Had
prompt_text: Talk about a particular morning that turned out to be especially memorable for you. Describe when and where it happened, and who you were with at the time. Tell us what made that morning so special, and explain how you felt as it unfolded.
template_hint: Open by setting the scene (when and where that specific morning happened and who was there), then narrate what actually happened in order, and finish by explaining why it stuck with you and how it made you feel.
sample_response_band12: One morning about a year ago, I woke up earlier than usual at our family cottage by the lake. At first I was a little annoyed because I had wanted to sleep in, but then I noticed how completely silent everything was. I quietly slipped outside with a warm cup of coffee and sat on the wooden dock while my parents were still sleeping. Suddenly the sun broke over the trees, and the whole lake turned a soft golden colour. A pair of loons drifted past, calling to each other, and I just sat there, totally still, feeling deeply peaceful. After a while my dad joined me, and we talked quietly about nothing in particular. Looking back, that calm, unexpected morning really mattered to me because it reminded me to slow down. It honestly set the tone for that entire summer, and I still smile whenever I remember it.

title: Helping Out At Home As A Child
prompt_text: Talk about a time when you helped out at home when you were a child. How old were you, and what task did you take on? Why did you decide to help, and what did you learn from that experience?
template_hint: Set the scene (how old you were and the situation at home), describe the task you took on and how it went, then end with the lesson it taught you.
sample_response_band12: When I was about eight years old, my mother caught a really bad cold and had to stay in bed for a few days. Seeing her so tired, I decided I wanted to lend a hand around the house. One day, I quietly took it upon myself to prepare a simple breakfast for my younger brother. So, I carefully toasted some bread, poured two glasses of milk, and even arranged everything neatly on a tray. As soon as my mom saw what I had done, she gave me the warmest smile and called me her little helper. After a while, I also started clearing the dishes and tidying up the living room. Honestly, I felt incredibly grown-up and proud of myself. Even now, I remember how rewarding it felt to take on real responsibility, and that experience taught me how good it feels to care for the people I love.

title: The Day You Got a Pet
prompt_text: Think about the day you got a pet for the first time. Describe that day to a friend. What kind of pet was it, and where did it come from? What was your very first impression, and how did your life change afterward?
template_hint: Set the scene (when you got the pet and where it came from), describe your first meeting and impression, then end with how the pet changed your life.
sample_response_band12: A few years ago, on a bright Saturday afternoon, my family and I drove to a local animal shelter to adopt a dog. The moment we walked in, this tiny golden puppy ran straight up to me and started wagging its tail like crazy. At first, I was nervous because I had never owned a pet before, but I instantly fell in love with those big, hopeful eyes. We named her Bella, and on the drive home she curled up in my lap and fell asleep. Soon, she became the most playful, affectionate companion I could imagine. From that day on, my whole daily routine changed. I had to wake up early for walks and feedings, but I never once minded. Ever since, Bella has filled our home with so much joy, and I genuinely can't picture my life without her.

title: A Sunrise Or Sunset You Loved
prompt_text: Talk about a sunrise or sunset that you will never forget. Describe a specific time when you watched the sky change colours and it left a strong impression on you. When and where did it happen? Who were you with, and why was that moment so memorable?
template_hint: Set the scene (when, where, who), describe how the sky slowly changed and how you felt watching it, then end with why that moment stayed with you.
sample_response_band12: I'll never forget a sunset I saw a couple of summers ago, when I visited my cousin who lives near the coast. It was a warm August evening, and the two of us decided to walk down to the pier just before eight. At first the sky was a plain, washed-out blue, but little by little it turned into these incredible shades of orange and deep pink. The sun looked enormous as it sank toward the horizon, and the whole bay was glowing. We just stood there in silence, watching the boats turn into dark silhouettes. Before I knew it, the sun had completely disappeared and the stars were coming out. Looking back, what made it so special wasn't only how breathtaking it was, but how calm and connected I felt in that moment with someone I love.

title: A Camping Trip You Went On
prompt_text: Talk about a camping trip that you went on. Describe a specific time when you stayed outdoors overnight and tell us what the experience was like. When did the trip happen, and who did you go with? What did you do there, and what made the trip stand out for you?
template_hint: Set the scene (when, where, who you camped with), walk through the main events of the trip in order, then close with why the trip was meaningful.
sample_response_band12: A few years ago, three of my closest friends and I went on a weekend camping trip to a national park up in the mountains. To start with, we spent almost an hour struggling to put up the tent, which had us laughing the whole time. After that, we hiked along a rocky trail and found this gorgeous lake where we ate our lunch. The best part came later that night, though. We built a campfire, roasted marshmallows, and told stories until really late. All of a sudden, we heard rustling in the bushes, and we were convinced a bear was coming, but it turned out to be a tiny raccoon. By the end of the trip, I felt completely refreshed. What made it so memorable was being totally disconnected from screens and just enjoying the wilderness with good company.

title: A Close Encounter With An Animal
prompt_text: Talk about a time when you had a close encounter with an animal. Describe a specific situation when an animal came surprisingly near to you. When and where did this happen, what kind of animal was it, and how did you react?
template_hint: Set the scene (when, where, what you were doing), build tension as the animal appears and you react, then reflect on how it felt and why it stuck with you.
sample_response_band12: One morning a couple of summers ago, I was jogging on a quiet trail near my home, just as the sun was coming up. Out of nowhere, a deer stepped onto the path only a few metres in front of me. I was completely startled and froze on the spot, my heart pounding. At that moment, the deer froze too, and we just stared at each other. For a few seconds, neither of us moved. I noticed how huge and gentle its eyes were, and I tried to breathe quietly so I wouldn't scare it. Eventually, it flicked its ears, turned, and bounded off into the trees. In the end, the whole thing lasted maybe twenty seconds, but it felt magical. I still remember it because it was a rare, peaceful reminder of how close wild creatures really are to us.

title: A Day At The Beach Or Lake
prompt_text: Talk about a day that you spent at a beach or a lake. Describe a specific day when you went to the water to relax or have fun. When did this happen, and who went with you? What did you do during the day, and why was it an enjoyable experience?
template_hint: Set the scene (when, where, who you went with), describe the activities you did during the day, then end with the overall feeling and why it was a good day.
sample_response_band12: Last summer, my whole family spent an entire Saturday at a lake about an hour from our city. First of all, we got there early and claimed a nice spot right on the shoreline before it got too crowded. Then my younger brother and I jumped straight into the water, and it was so refreshing after the long, hot drive. Throughout the day, we swam, built a giant sandcastle, and floated around on an inflatable raft. My parents mostly relaxed, sunbathing and reading under a big umbrella. Just as the afternoon was ending, we had a picnic with sandwiches and watermelon right by the water. All in all, it was such a simple but perfect day. What made it special was that everyone in my family was relaxed and happy at the same time, which honestly doesn't happen very often.

title: Growing A Plant Or Garden
prompt_text: Talk about a time when you grew a plant or a garden of your own. Describe one specific experience of looking after something you were growing. When did you start, and what did you decide to grow? How did you take care of it, and why was the experience rewarding for you?
template_hint: Set the scene (when and why you started, what you grew), describe the process of caring for it over time, then end with the payoff and how it made you feel.
sample_response_band12: A while back, during a long, slow period when I was stuck at home, I decided to grow tomatoes on my small balcony. In the beginning, I honestly had no idea what I was doing, so I just planted a few seeds in some pots of soil and hoped for the best. Day by day, I watered them every morning and made sure they got plenty of sunlight. After a few weeks, tiny green sprouts finally pushed through, and I was so excited I actually took photos of them. Eventually, the plants grew tall and started to blossom, and small green tomatoes appeared. In the end, I harvested a whole bowl of ripe, red tomatoes and used them in a salad for my family. It was incredibly rewarding because I had created something from almost nothing, and it taught me to be patient.

title: A Walk That Cleared Your Mind
prompt_text: Talk about a walk that completely cleared your mind. Describe a specific time when going for a walk helped you calm down or think more clearly about a problem. When did this happen, and where did you walk? What was on your mind beforehand, and how did you feel by the end of the walk?
template_hint: Set the scene (when, where, what was stressing you), describe how the walk gradually changed your mood, then end with the clear-headed feeling you were left with.
sample_response_band12: One evening, after a really stressful week at work, I felt completely overwhelmed, so I decided to go for a long walk in a park near my apartment. Beforehand, my head was spinning with deadlines and a difficult conversation I'd had with my boss, and I just couldn't switch off. As I walked, though, I started to notice the fresh air, the sound of the leaves, and people walking their dogs. Step by step, my breathing slowed down and my thoughts stopped racing. Gradually, the problems that had felt enormous an hour earlier began to seem much more manageable. By the time I got home, almost an hour later, I felt completely clear-headed and at ease. I remember that walk because it taught me something simple but important, which is that sometimes the best way to solve a problem is just to step away from it.

title: A Storm or Snowfall You Remember
prompt_text: Talk about a storm or a snowfall that you remember vividly. Describe a specific time when extreme weather made a strong impression on you. When and where did it happen, and what was the weather like? What did you do during it, and why has it stayed in your memory?
template_hint: Set the scene (when, where, who you were with), describe the dramatic weather and what you did during it, then close with why the memory has lasted.
sample_response_band12: I remember one winter, a few years ago, when an enormous snowstorm hit our city overnight. It started as a light snowfall in the evening, but suddenly, around midnight, the wind picked up and turned it into a full blizzard. Within minutes, you couldn't see the houses across the street, and the howling wind was almost deafening. While the storm raged outside, my family and I sat together in the living room with candles, because the power had gone out. We played board games and drank hot chocolate to stay warm. After it passed, we woke up to find everything buried under a thick, white blanket of snow, and the whole neighbourhood was silent and sparkling. To this day, I remember it so clearly because, even though it was a little scary, it brought my family closer together in a really cozy way.

title: A Night Looking At The Stars
prompt_text: Talk about a night that you spent looking at the stars. Describe a specific time when you watched the night sky and it made an impression on you. When and where did this happen, and who were you with? What did you see, and why was that night memorable for you?
template_hint: Set the scene (when, where, who you were with), describe what you saw in the sky and how you reacted, then end with the feeling or thought it left you with.
sample_response_band12: One night, when I was staying at a friend's cabin far from the city, the two of us decided to lie on a blanket in the backyard and look up at the stars. At first my eyes hadn't adjusted, so I could only see a few faint dots. After a while, though, as it got truly pitch black, hundreds of twinkling stars appeared, and I could even make out the faint band of the Milky Way stretching across the sky. Just then a shooting star streaked across, and we both gasped and made a wish. Eventually we stopped talking and simply lay there in silence for what felt like an hour. Even now, I remember how tiny and yet how peaceful I felt. The sky was so vast that it really put all my everyday worries into perspective.

title: A Memorable Meal With Loved Ones
prompt_text: Talk about a memorable meal you shared with family or friends. Describe a specific time when you sat down to eat together and it really stayed with you. When and where did it happen? Who was there with you, and what made that meal so special?
template_hint: Set the scene with when, where, and who, then walk through what you ate and talked about, and close with why that meal still feels special to you.
sample_response_band12: A couple of years ago, my whole family got together at my grandmother's small apartment to celebrate her birthday. It was a chilly winter evening, and the kitchen was warm and steamy from all the cooking. We squeezed around her old wooden table, maybe ten of us, elbow to elbow. As soon as she brought out her famous chicken stew, everyone went quiet for a second, and then the laughter started. My cousins and I kept telling old childhood stories, and my grandmother just sat there smiling. Before long, we'd been at that table for almost three hours. Honestly, the food was delicious, but it wasn't really about the food. Looking back now, that meal stays with me because it was one of the last big gatherings we had before my grandmother passed away. I still feel that warmth whenever I think about it.

title: Returning A Faulty Product
prompt_text: Talk about a time when you returned a faulty product or made a complaint about poor service. What was the product or service, and what exactly went wrong? When did this happen, and who did you speak to about the problem? Describe how the situation was resolved and how you felt about it.
connector_phrases: As soon as I got home
template_hint: Explain what you bought and what was wrong with it, describe the conversation when you complained, and finish with how it was resolved and how you felt.
sample_response_band12: A while back, I bought a pair of expensive wireless headphones from an electronics store as a treat for myself. As soon as I got home and charged them, I realized the left side wasn't working at all. The problem was that I had thrown away the box, so I was worried they wouldn't take them back. The next day I returned to the store, found my receipt, and explained the situation to the customer service desk. At first the clerk seemed reluctant, but after I calmly described the defect, he tested them and agreed they were clearly faulty. Eventually he offered me a full refund or a brand-new pair, and I chose the exchange. To my relief, the new ones worked perfectly. It was memorable because I'm usually shy about complaining, and that day I learned that staying polite but firm really does get results.

title: A Wonderful Restaurant Experience
prompt_text: Talk about a wonderful experience you once had at a restaurant. Think of a specific time when everything about the visit felt just right. Where was the restaurant, and what was the occasion? What happened during the meal that made it so memorable for you?
connector_phrases: By the end of the night
template_hint: Set the scene with the restaurant and the occasion, describe the standout moments of service or food, and finish with why it left such a strong impression.
sample_response_band12: A few months ago, my partner took me to a small Italian restaurant downtown to celebrate our anniversary. The moment we walked in, the lighting was soft and warm, and a waiter greeted us by name because my partner had booked ahead. Throughout the evening, the service was incredibly attentive without being pushy. The waiter quietly recommended a pasta dish that turned out to be the best I had ever tasted. What really stood out, though, was that the chef came out personally to wish us a happy anniversary and brought us a free dessert with a little candle. By the end of the night, I felt completely relaxed and genuinely cared for. Ever since then, that restaurant has become our special place, and we go back every year. It taught me how much a few thoughtful touches can turn a simple dinner into something unforgettable.

title: Looking After A Child
prompt_text: Talk about a time when you looked after a child or a relative for a day. Who did you take care of, and why were you the one responsible that day? When and where did this happen, and what did you do together? Explain what the experience was like and how you felt by the end of the day.
template_hint: Explain who you were caring for and why, describe the activities and any challenges during the day, then reflect on how the experience left you feeling.
sample_response_band12: One Saturday, my sister asked me to look after my five-year-old nephew for the whole day while she went to a wedding. The moment she left, I realized I had my hands full, because he was full of energy and wanted to play nonstop. Throughout the day we built a fort out of blankets, drew pictures, and went to the park near my apartment. At one point he scraped his knee, and I had to stay calm and clean it up while he cried. By the afternoon, I was completely worn out, but he finally settled down for a nap, which gave me a moment to breathe. By the end of it, I had a brand-new respect for my sister and how much energy parenting takes. It was exhausting, but it was also one of the most rewarding days I've had in a long time.

title: Sharing Food From My Culture
prompt_text: Talk about a time when you introduced someone to a food from your culture. Describe a specific occasion when you shared a traditional dish with a person who had never tried it. Who was that person, and what did you give them to try? How did they react, and how did it make you feel?
template_hint: Introduce the person and the dish, describe their reaction as they tasted it, and reflect on what it meant to you to share part of your heritage.
sample_response_band12: A while back, I invited my Canadian roommate to try samosas, which are a traditional snack from my home country. She had never even heard of them, so I decided to make them from scratch one weekend. At first, she was a little hesitant because the spices smelled stronger than what she was used to. I explained how my mother used to fry them every festival, and that calmed her nerves. Once she took a bite, her whole expression changed, and she immediately asked for a second one. To my delight, she said it was now her favourite snack in the world. After that, she even asked me to teach her the recipe so she could make them for her own family. Thinking back, sharing that little piece of my heritage made me feel proud and surprisingly close to home, even though I was thousands of miles away.

title: An Unforgettable Live Event You Watched
prompt_text: Talk about a time when you went to watch a live concert, show, or sports match as a member of the audience. Describe what you saw and what made the experience stand out for you. When and where did it happen? Who did you go with, and why do you still remember it?
template_hint: Open by setting the scene (when, where, and who you went with), build up to the most exciting moment of the event with vivid sensory detail, then close with how the atmosphere made you feel and why it stuck with you.
sample_response_band12: A couple of years ago, I went to my very first live football match with my older brother in our city's main stadium. We got there early, and the place was already packed with thousands of fans wearing the team's colours. The moment our team scored, the whole crowd jumped up at once and let out this deafening roar, and honestly, I felt the ground shake under my feet. My brother and I were hugging complete strangers and singing along to the chants. As soon as the final whistle blew, we had won by a single goal, and the celebration was incredible. By the end of the night, my voice was completely gone from all the cheering. I'll never forget that atmosphere, because it was the first time I truly felt part of something so big and electric.

title: A Meal With A Stranger
prompt_text: Talk about a time when you shared a meal with someone you had just met. Describe a specific occasion when you ended up eating with a person who was a stranger to you at first. Where were you, and how did you come to share that meal? What did you talk about, and how did the experience turn out?
connector_phrases: By the end of the meal
template_hint: Explain how you ended up eating with a stranger, describe how the conversation developed, and finish with how the connection or the experience surprised you.
sample_response_band12: During a solo trip to Vietnam last year, I sat down at a tiny street-food stall, and the only free seat was next to an older local man eating alone. At first, it felt a little awkward because we didn't share a common language. But as we started talking, mostly through gestures and a translation app, we found a rhythm. He insisted on showing me how to wrap my spring rolls properly, and he even ordered me a local drink I'd never heard of. Little by little, we exchanged stories about our families and our jobs. By the end of the meal, he'd paid for my food and refused to let me argue about it. Surprisingly, what could have been an awkward dinner alone turned into one of the warmest moments of my whole trip. It reminded me that kindness really does cross every language barrier.

title: Decluttering Your Living Space
prompt_text: Talk about a time when you reorganized, cleaned out, or decluttered your living space. What part of your home did you tackle, and why did you decide to do it then? When did this happen, and did anyone help you? Describe what you got rid of and how you felt once the space was finished.
template_hint: Explain what motivated you to clean out the space, describe the process of sorting and discarding items, then share the satisfying result and how it felt.
sample_response_band12: Last spring, I finally decided to tackle my bedroom closet, which had become so cluttered I could barely close the door. It all started when I couldn't find a jacket I knew was somewhere in that mess. That weekend, my roommate offered to help, so we pulled everything out and made three piles: keep, donate, and throw away. Little by little, we sorted through years of clothes I had completely forgotten about. After a few hours, I was amazed at how much I no longer needed, and we filled four big bags to donate to a local charity. Once everything was done, the closet looked twice as big and felt so organized. Ever since then, I've kept it tidy and found it much easier to get ready in the mornings. It stuck with me because clearing out that space honestly felt like a fresh start for my whole life.

title: Losing And Finding Something
prompt_text: Talk about a time when you lost something important and then found it again, or someone returned it to you. What was the item, and how did you come to lose it? When and where did this happen, and what did you do to try to find it? Explain how you felt when it finally came back to you.
connector_phrases: Just as I was giving up
I can't tell you how relieved
template_hint: Describe the important item and how you lost it, build tension through your search, then deliver the relief of getting it back and why it mattered.
sample_response_band12: A few months ago, I lost my wallet on my way home from work, and it had my ID, bank cards, and some cash inside. At first I assumed I had simply left it on my desk, but when I checked the next morning, it wasn't there. My heart sank when I realized I must have dropped it somewhere between the office and the train station. I spent that whole evening retracing my steps and even called the transit lost-and-found, but nothing turned up. Just as I was giving up hope, I got a phone call from a stranger who had found my wallet on the sidewalk and tracked me down through a card inside. It turned out that everything was still there, untouched. I can't tell you how relieved and grateful I felt. That moment really restored my faith in people's honesty, and I still think about that kind stranger today.
